content_en:
In his hadith to the delegation from the people of ‘Abd al-Qays, he [the Prophet] interpreted Iman in a way similar to his interpretation of Islam (as illustrated above), with only one exception. Pilgrimage was not mentioned in his interpretation of Iman, even though it is generally agreed that the pilgrimage is part of belief. He said: “I command you to have belief only in Allah. Do you really know what belief only in Allah is?” They replied: “Allah and His Messenger know best.” He said: “Testifying that there is no god but Allah and Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah, performing prayers, giving zakah, fasting during Ramadan, and paying one-fifth of the spoils that you acquire.”

The Prophet said: “Modesty is a branch of Iman.” This was affirmed by many narrators, such as [Abd Allah] Ibn “Umar,” Ibn Ma‘siid,> and ‘Imran Ibn Husayn. He [the Prophet] also said: “None of you is a believer until | am dearer to him than his child, his parents, and all people.” He said: “None among you truly believes until one loves for his brother that which one loves for himself.” He also said: “I swear by Allah that one never has belief, I swear by Allah that one never has belief, I swear by Allah that one never has belief.” The Prophet repeated it three times. He was asked: “Oh, Prophet! Who is that?” The Prophet replied: “He whose neighbor is not secure from his harmful conduct.” The Prophet also said: “If anyone among you should see something wrong, he should correct it with his hand; and if he is not able to, then with this tongue; if he is not able to, then with his heart. And that is the weakest of Iman.” And the Prophet said: “Never before has a Prophet been sent by Allah to his ummah, community, who among his people had no disciples and companions who followed his ways and obeyed his commands. Then after them come their successors, who say one thing but do another, and practiced not what they were commanded to do. Thus, he who strove against them with his hand is considered a believer; and he who strove against them with his tongue is considered a believer; and he who strove against them

> Abu Sa‘id al-Khudri, Sa‘d Ibn Malik (2-74 A.H.), was considered one of the main narrators of hadith from the Prophet. He felt that hadiths should not be written down and forbid his students to do so. [Al-Zirikli, vol. 3, p. 150 and M. M. Azami, p. 39.]

*4 “abd Allah Abu ‘Abd al-Rahman Ibn “Umar Ibn al-Khattab (?-73 A.H.) was the son of the second Caliph, an excellent transmitter of hadith and was considered to be a model of a pious person. (E/ 2, vol. 1, pp. 53-54 and al-Zirikli, vol. 4, p. 246.]

25 “Abd Allah Ibn Mas‘dd (?-32 A.H.) was a famous Companion of the Prophet and a reciter of

THE DIFFERENCE BETWEEN IMAN, ISLAM AND IHSAN 27

with his heart, he is also considered a believer. Beyond that there is no belief even to the size of a habbat khardal, mustard seed” (narrated by Muslim only). .

Muslim also narrated that the Prophet said: “By Him in whose hand is my soul, you will never enter Paradise until you believe and you will never believe until you love one another. Should I not direct you to a thing which, if you do it, will foster love among you? Practice frequently saying among you, al-salému ‘alaykum, peace be upon you.” He [the Prophet] said in the hadith whose narration is agreed upon and narrated by Abu Hurayrah. Al-Bukhari reported the hadith of Ibn ‘Abbas* that the Prophet (pbuh) said: “The fornicator who fornicates is not a believer as long as he commits it; a drunkard who drinks wine is not a believer as long as he drinks it; no thief who steals is a believer as long as he commits theft; and no plunderer who plunders al-nuhbah, the valuable thing, that which attracts the attention of people, is a believer as long as he commits this act.”

IMAN AND ISLAM AS CITED IN THE QUR?AN

The word Iman is cited in the Qur?dn by itself and not in conjunction with the word Islam nor with the phrase ‘righteous work’ or other such words or phrases. Sometimes the word Iman is cited as being linked to the word Islam as indicated in Gabriel’s hadith: “What is Islam and what is Iman?” and in Allah’s saying: “ Indeed Muslim men and women, and believing men and women . . .” (33:35) and He says: “AI-A ‘rab, the desert Arabs, say: ‘We believe.’ Say: ‘You have no faith’; but you only say: ‘We have submitted our wills to Allah . . .”” (49:14); Allah also says: “So We brought out from therein the believers who were there. But We found only one house with Muslims in it” (51:35~36). The word Iman was also mentioned in conjunction with the phrase “righteous works,” as indicated in the many verses of the Qur’an. For example, Allah, the Exalted, says: “Indeed, those who have belief and do righteous works . . .” (2:277).

The word Iman may also be linked to those endowed with knowledge. Allah says: “But those endowed with knowledge and belief will say . . .” (30:56), Allah also

** Tbn ‘Abbas, Abu al-‘Abbas ‘Abd Allah (?~68 A.H.), was considered one of the greatest scholars of the first Islamic generation. He was also regarded as the father of Qur’nic exegesis. [Al-

says: “.. . Allah will raise up, to suitable ranks and degrees, those of you who believe and who have been granted knowledge . . .” (58:11). Whenever He mentions those who believe in Him, those who are endowed with knowledge are included, for they are the best of believers. Allah says: “. . . And those who are firmly grounded in knowledge say: ‘We believe in it [the Book]; the whole of it is from our Lord . . .”” (3:7). Allah also says: “. . . Those among them who are well-grounded in knowledge, and the believers, believe in what has been revealed to you and what was revealed before you . . .” (4:162).

The word al-mu’miniin, believers, is also mentioned in the Qur’4n in conjunction with those who follow the Jewish, Christian, and Sabian [religions]. Allah says: “Indeed! Those who believe and those who are Jews and Christians, and Sabians,”” whosoever believes in Allah and the Last Day and does righteous work, shall have their reward with their Lord, on them shall be no fear, nor shall they grieve” (2:62).* Thus [Allah differentiates], in the first part of the verse, between the believers and the other three groups. However, in the second part of the verse, “whosoever believes in Allah,” the word believe is generalized to speak of those who believe in the Qur’an, who follow the Jewish scriptures, the Christians, and the Sabians. This generalization is also cited in Allah’s saying: “Indeed those who believe and do righteous works are the best of creatures” (98:7). This issue will be explained fully later, in sha’a Allah.

The intended difference here is a matter of generalization and particularization of the external and internal aspects of Iman. However, the generalization regarding the milals, religious communities (as cited in 2:62)” is a different issue. Whenever Allah mentions Iman jointly with Islam, He regards the latter as the

2?’ The name Sabians occurs in three different verses in the Qur’&n-2:62, 5:69 and 22:17. It is a religious community whose several thousand probable descendants live in Southern Iraq on the banks of the Euphrates and Tigris Rivers. They dress in white and believe in frequent immersion in water and are often called baptisers. Their religious book is Ginza and is written in the Aramaic language. There was another group also referred to by the name Sabians of Haran, a city today belongs to Turkey. They were star worshipers and famous astrologers and had hellenistic tendencies. E/: Vol.8, 675-79 and Fihrist: Vol.2, 745-72.

28 The text of Kitab al-Iman does not quote the first part of this verse, "shall have their reward with their Lord." However, the discussion that follows makes it necessary to quote the entire verse. Also, in all the recensions of Kitab al-Iman consulted, this verse is cited as Strat al-Ma’idah, verse number 69. The correct reference is Surat al-Bagarah, verse 62, as cited above.

THE DIFFERENCE BETWEEN IMAN, ISLAM AND IHSAN 29

external works (i.e., testifying that there is no god but Allah and that Muhammad is His Messenger, performing prayers, giving zakah, fasting during Ramadan, and performing the pilgrimage). However, He regards Iman as that which is at the heart of belief in Allah, His Angels, His Books, His Messengers, and in the Day of Judgment. This difference is affirmed in the hadith narrated by Ahmad [Ibn Hanbal] on the authority of Anas from the Prophet (pbuh), who said: “Islam is explicit and Iman is in the heart.”

Thus whenever the word Iman is cited alone it implies both Islam and righteous works, as is mentioned in the hadith of the branches of Iman which says: “Iman consists of more than seventy branches, the highest of which is saying, La ilaha illa Allah, there is no god but Allah, and the least of which is the removing of harm from the road.” This applies to all sayings of the Prophet in which all righteous works are mentioned as part of Iman.

If Iman is negated by abandoning [righteous works], then they are considered as obligations for Iman. However, if the virtue of someone whose righteousness is mentioned, provided that his belief is not negated, then these [righteous works] are considered to be desirable for Iman. For Allah and His Messenger do not negate an obligation they command unless one fails to perform part of that obligation. For example, the Prophet said: “No prayer is complete without reciting umm al-Qur’4n, the opening chapter, and no true belief can be ascribed to one who has no loyalty, and no religion is complete for the one who does not abide by his commitment.”
content_id:
Dalam haditsnya kepada delegasi dari kaum 'Abd al-Qays, beliau [Nabi] menafsirkan Iman dengan cara yang serupa dengan penafsirannya tentang Islam (sebagaimana diilustrasikan di atas), hanya dengan satu pengecualian. Haji tidak disebutkan dalam penafsirannya tentang Iman, meskipun secara umum disepakati bahwa haji merupakan bagian dari keyakinan. Beliau bersabda: "Aku memerintahkan kalian untuk beriman hanya kepada Allah. Apakah kalian benar-benar mengetahui apa itu iman hanya kepada Allah?" Mereka menjawab: "Allah dan Rasul-Nya lebih mengetahui." Beliau bersabda: "Bersaksi bahwa tidak ada tuhan selain Allah dan Muhammad adalah Rasul Allah, mendirikan salah, membayar zakah, berpuasa di bulan Ramadan, dan membayar seperlima dari ghanimah yang kalian peroleh."

Nabi bersabda: "Malu adalah cabang dari Iman." Hal ini ditegaskan oleh banyak perawi, seperti [Abd Allah] Ibn 'Umar, Ibn Mas'ud, dan 'Imran Ibn Husayn. Beliau [Nabi] juga bersabda: "Tidak seorang pun di antara kalian yang beriman hingga aku lebih dicintainya daripada anaknya, orang tuanya, dan semua orang." Beliau bersabda: "Tidak seorang pun di antara kalian yang benar-benar beriman hingga ia mencintai untuk saudaranya apa yang ia cintai untuk dirinya sendiri." Beliau juga bersabda: "Demi Allah, ia tidak beriman, demi Allah ia tidak beriman, demi Allah ia tidak beriman." Nabi mengulanginya tiga kali. Beliau ditanya: "Wahai Nabi! Siapakah orang itu?" Nabi menjawab: "Ia yang tetangganya tidak aman dari perbuatan buruknya." Nabi juga bersabda: "Jika seseorang di antara kalian melihat suatu kemungkaran, hendaklah ia mengubahnya dengan tangannya; jika tidak mampu, maka dengan lisannya; jika tidak mampu, maka dengan hatinya. Dan itulah selemah-lemah Iman." Dan Nabi bersabda: "Tidak pernah sebelumnya seorang Nabi diutus oleh Allah kepada umatnya, yang di antara kaumnya tidak memiliki murid dan sahabat yang mengikuti jalannya dan mematuhi perintahnya. Kemudian setelah mereka datanglah penerus mereka, yang mengatakan satu hal namun berbuat yang lain, dan tidak mengamalkan apa yang mereka diperintahkan. Maka, barangsiapa yang berjuang melawan mereka dengan tangannya dianggap sebagai orang yang beriman; dan barangsiapa yang berjuang melawan mereka dengan lisannya dianggap sebagai orang yang beriman; dan barangsiapa yang berjuang melawan mereka

> Abu Sa'id al-Khudri, Sa'd Ibn Malik (2-74 H.), dianggap sebagai salah satu perawi utama hadits dari Nabi. Ia berpendapat bahwa hadits tidak seharusnya ditulis dan melarang murid-muridnya untuk melakukannya. [Al-Zirikli, vol. 3, hal. 150 dan M. M. Azami, hal. 39.]

*4 'Abd Allah Abu 'Abd al-Rahman Ibn 'Umar Ibn al-Khattab (?-73 H.) adalah putra Khalifah kedua, perawi hadits yang sangat baik dan dianggap sebagai teladan orang yang saleh. [EI 2, vol. 1, hal. 53-54 dan al-Zirikli, vol. 4, hal. 246.]

25 'Abd Allah Ibn Mas'ud (?-32 H.) adalah Sahabat Nabi yang terkenal dan seorang qari

PERBEDAAN ANTARA IMAN, ISLAM DAN IHSAN 27

dengan hatinya juga dianggap sebagai orang yang beriman. Di luar itu tidak ada iman meskipun sebesar habbat khardal, biji sawi" (diriwayatkan oleh Muslim saja).

Muslim juga meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi bersabda: "Demi Dzat yang jiwaku berada di tangan-Nya, kalian tidak akan masuk surga hingga kalian beriman, dan kalian tidak akan beriman hingga kalian saling mencintai. Maukah aku tunjukkan kepada kalian sesuatu yang jika kalian lakukan akan menumbuhkan rasa cinta di antara kalian? Perbanyaklah mengucapkan di antara kalian, al-salāmu 'alaykum, semoga keselamatan tercurah atas kalian." Beliau [Nabi] bersabda dalam hadits yang disepakati periwayatannya dan diriwayatkan oleh Abu Hurayrah. Al-Bukhari meriwayatkan hadits Ibn 'Abbas bahwa Nabi (saw) bersabda: "Pezina yang berzina bukanlah seorang mukmin selama ia melakukannya; pemabuk yang meminum khamr bukanlah seorang mukmin selama ia meminumnya; pencuri yang mencuri bukanlah seorang mukmin selama ia melakukan pencurian; dan perampas yang merampas al-nuhbah, sesuatu yang berharga, yang menarik perhatian orang, bukanlah seorang mukmin selama ia melakukan perbuatan ini."

IMAN DAN ISLAM SEBAGAIMANA DISEBUTKAN DALAM AL-QUR'AN

Kata Iman disebutkan dalam al-Qur'an secara tersendiri dan tidak bersamaan dengan kata Islam maupun dengan frasa 'amal saleh' atau kata-kata atau frasa lain semacamnya. Kadang-kadang kata Iman disebutkan sebagai terkait dengan kata Islam sebagaimana ditunjukkan dalam hadits Jibril: "Apa itu Islam dan apa itu Iman?" dan dalam firman Allah: "Sesungguhnya laki-laki dan perempuan Muslim, dan laki-laki dan perempuan mukmin . . ." (33:35) dan Dia berfirman: "Al-A'rab, orang-orang Arab Badui, berkata: 'Kami telah beriman.' Katakanlah: 'Kamu tidak beriman'; tetapi katakanlah: 'Kami telah tunduk (masuk Islam) . . .'" (49:14); Allah juga berfirman: "Maka Kami keluarkan dari sana orang-orang mukmin yang ada di sana. Namun Kami hanya menemukan satu rumah yang penghuninya Muslim" (51:35-36). Kata Iman juga disebutkan bersamaan dengan frasa "amal saleh," sebagaimana ditunjukkan dalam banyak ayat al-Qur'an. Misalnya, Allah Yang Maha Agung berfirman: "Sesungguhnya orang-orang yang beriman dan mengerjakan amal saleh . . ." (2:277).

Kata Iman juga dapat dikaitkan dengan mereka yang dianugerahi ilmu. Allah berfirman: "Tetapi orang-orang yang dianugerahi ilmu dan iman akan berkata . . ." (30:56), Allah juga

** Ibn 'Abbas, Abu al-'Abbas 'Abd Allah (?-68 H.), dianggap sebagai salah satu ulama terbesar generasi Islam pertama. Ia juga dipandang sebagai bapak tafsir al-Qur'an. [Al-

berfirman: ". . . Allah akan meninggikan orang-orang yang beriman di antara kamu dan orang-orang yang diberi ilmu pengetahuan beberapa derajat . . ." (58:11). Setiap kali Dia menyebutkan orang-orang yang beriman kepada-Nya, maka orang-orang yang dianugerahi ilmu termasuk di dalamnya, karena mereka adalah sebaik-baik orang yang beriman. Allah berfirman: ". . . Dan orang-orang yang mendalam ilmunya berkata: 'Kami beriman dengan ayat-ayat yang mutasyabihat; semuanya itu dari sisi Tuhan kami . . .'" (3:7). Allah juga berfirman: ". . . Tetapi orang-orang yang mendalam ilmunya di antara mereka dan orang-orang mukmin, mereka beriman kepada apa yang telah diturunkan kepadamu dan apa yang telah diturunkan sebelummu . . ." (4:162).

Kata al-mu'minūn, orang-orang yang beriman, juga disebutkan dalam al-Qur'an bersamaan dengan orang-orang yang mengikuti agama Yahudi, Kristen, dan Sabi'. Allah berfirman: "Sesungguhnya orang-orang mukmin, orang-orang Yahudi, orang-orang Nasrani, dan orang-orang Sabi'," barangsiapa beriman kepada Allah dan hari akhir dan mengerjakan amal saleh, maka mereka mendapat pahala di sisi Tuhan mereka, tidak ada kekhawatiran atas mereka, dan tidak pula mereka bersedih hati" (2:62).* Dengan demikian [Allah membedakan], pada bagian pertama ayat tersebut, antara orang-orang mukmin dan tiga kelompok lainnya. Namun pada bagian kedua ayat tersebut, "barangsiapa beriman kepada Allah," kata beriman digeneralisasikan untuk berbicara tentang orang-orang yang beriman kepada al-Qur'an, yang mengikuti kitab suci Yahudi, orang-orang Nasrani, dan orang-orang Sabi'. Generalisasi ini juga disebutkan dalam firman Allah: "Sesungguhnya orang-orang yang beriman dan mengerjakan amal saleh, mereka itu adalah sebaik-baik makhluk" (98:7). Masalah ini akan dijelaskan sepenuhnya nanti, in sha'a Allah.

Perbedaan yang dimaksud di sini adalah masalah generalisasi dan pengkhususan aspek lahir dan batin dari Iman. Namun, generalisasi mengenai milal, komunitas agama (sebagaimana disebutkan dalam 2:62) merupakan masalah yang berbeda. Setiap kali Allah menyebutkan Iman bersamaan dengan Islam, Dia memandang yang terakhir sebagai

27 Nama Sabi' disebutkan dalam tiga ayat berbeda dalam al-Qur'an — 2:62, 5:69, dan 22:17. Ini adalah komunitas agama yang beberapa ribu keturunan mereka yang mungkin masih ada tinggal di Irak Selatan di tepi sungai Efrat dan Tigris. Mereka berpakaian putih dan percaya pada pencelupan diri ke dalam air yang sering dilakukan dan sering disebut sebagai kaum pembaptis. Kitab suci mereka adalah Ginza dan ditulis dalam bahasa Aram. Ada kelompok lain yang juga disebut dengan nama Sabi' Haran, sebuah kota yang sekarang termasuk wilayah Turki. Mereka adalah penyembah bintang dan ahli astrologi terkenal serta memiliki kecenderungan Helenistik. EI: Vol.8, 675-79 dan Fihrist: Vol.2, 745-72.

28 Teks Kitab al-Iman tidak mengutip bagian pertama ayat ini, "mereka mendapat pahala di sisi Tuhan mereka." Namun, pembahasan yang menyusul menjadikan kutipan seluruh ayat diperlukan. Juga, dalam semua versi Kitab al-Iman yang dikonsultasikan, ayat ini dikutip sebagai Surat al-Ma'idah, nomor ayat 69. Referensi yang benar adalah Surat al-Baqarah, ayat 62, sebagaimana dikutip di atas.

PERBEDAAN ANTARA IMAN, ISLAM DAN IHSAN 29

amal lahiriah (yaitu, bersaksi bahwa tidak ada tuhan selain Allah dan bahwa Muhammad adalah Rasul-Nya, mendirikan salah, membayar zakah, berpuasa di bulan Ramadan, dan melaksanakan haji). Namun Dia memandang Iman sebagai sesuatu yang ada di inti keyakinan kepada Allah, Malaikat-Nya, Kitab-Kitab-Nya, Rasul-Rasul-Nya, dan Hari Kiamat. Perbedaan ini ditegaskan dalam hadits yang diriwayatkan oleh Ahmad [Ibn Hanbal] dari Anas dari Nabi (saw), yang bersabda: "Islam itu tampak nyata dan Iman itu ada di dalam hati."

Dengan demikian, setiap kali kata iman disebutkan sendiri, ia mencakup Islam dan amal saleh sekaligus, sebagaimana disebutkan dalam hadith tentang cabang-cabang iman yang berbunyi: "Iman terdiri dari lebih dari tujuh puluh cabang, yang tertinggi adalah mengucapkan, La ilaha illa Allah, tiada tuhan selain Allah, dan yang paling rendah adalah menyingkirkan gangguan dari jalan." Hal ini berlaku untuk semua sabda Nabi di mana seluruh amal saleh disebutkan sebagai bagian dari iman.

Jika iman dinafikan karena meninggalkan [amal saleh], maka amal-amal tersebut dianggap sebagai kewajiban bagi iman. Namun, jika keutamaan seseorang yang amal salehnya disebutkan, selama imannya tidak dinafikan, maka [amal saleh] tersebut dianggap sebagai hal yang dianjurkan bagi iman. Karena Allah dan Rasul-Nya tidak menafikan suatu kewajiban yang mereka perintahkan kecuali seseorang gagal menunaikan sebagian dari kewajiban tersebut. Misalnya, Nabi bersabda: "Tidak sempurna salah seorang dari kalian sehingga ia mencintai untuk saudaranya apa yang ia cintai untuk dirinya sendiri, dan tidak ada shalat yang sempurna tanpa membaca umm al-Qur'an, yaitu surah pembuka, dan tidak dapat disebut benar-benar beriman orang yang tidak memiliki loyalitas, serta tidak sempurna agama orang yang tidak memenuhi komitmennya."
content_ar: In his الحديث to the delegation from the people of ‘Abd al-Qays, he [النبي ﷺ] interpreted الإيمان in a الطريق similar to his interpretation of الإسلام (as illustrated above), with only one exception. Pilgrimage was not mentioned in his interpretation of الإيمان, حتى وإن it is generally agreed that the pilgrimage is part of الاعتقاد. قال: “I command you to have الاعتقاد only in الله. Do you really know what الاعتقاد only in الله is?” They replied: “الله and His Messenger know best.” قال: “Testifying that there is no god but الله and محمد ﷺ is الرسول ﷺ of الله, performing الصلوات, giving الزكاة, الصيام خلال Ramadan, and paying one-خامساً of the spoils that you acquire.” النبي ﷺ said: “الحياء is a branch of الإيمان.” This was affirmed by many narrators, مثل [Abd الله] Ibn “عمر,” Ibn Ma‘siid,> and ‘Imran Ibn Husayn. He [النبي ﷺ] أيضاً said: “None of you is a المؤمن حتى | am dearer to him than his الولد, his الوالدان, and all people.” قال: “None among you truly believes حتى one loves for his الأخ that which one loves for himself.” He أيضاً said: “I swear by الله that one never has الاعتقاد, I swear by الله that one never has الاعتقاد, I swear by الله that one never has الاعتقاد.” النبي ﷺ repeated it three times. He was asked: “Oh, النبي! Who is that?” النبي ﷺ replied: “He whose الجار is not secure from his harmful السلوك.” النبي ﷺ أيضاً said: “إذا anyone among you should see something الخطأ, he should correct it with his hand; and إذا he is not able to, then with this tongue; إذا he is not able to, then with his القلب. And that is the weakest of الإيمان.” And النبي ﷺ said: “Never قبل has a النبي been sent by الله to his الأمة, الجماعة, who among his people had no disciples and الصحابة who followed his ways and obeyed his commands. Then بعد them come their التابعين, who say one thing but do another, and practiced not what they were commanded to do. وهكذا, he who strove against them with his hand is considered a المؤمن; and he who strove against them with his tongue is considered a المؤمن; and he who strove against them > Abu Sa‘id al-Khudri, Sa‘d Ibn مالك (2-74 A.H.), was considered one of the الرئيسي narrators of الحديث from النبي ﷺ. He felt that hadiths should not be written down and forbid his students to do لذا. [Al-Zirikli, vol. 3, p. 150 and M. M. Azami, p. 39.] *4 “abd الله Abu ‘Abd الرحمن Ibn “عمر Ibn al-Khattab (?-73 A.H.) was the son of the ثانياً Caliph, an excellent transmitter of الحديث and was considered to be a model of a pious person. (E/ 2, vol. 1, pp. 53-54 and al-Zirikli, vol. 4, p. 246.] 25 “Abd الله Ibn Mas‘dd (?-32 A.H.) was a famous Companion of النبي ﷺ and a reciter of THE DIFFERENCE BETWEEN الإيمان, الإسلام AND IHSAN 27 with his القلب, he is أيضاً considered a المؤمن. Beyond that there is no الاعتقاد even to the size of a habbat khardal, mustard seed” (narrated by مسلم only). . مسلم also n
